description:
src/lib.rs:319-325: 'std::str::from_utf8(value.as_bytes()).map(str::to_owned).map_err(|_| SecretError::DecodeFailed("Azure Key Vault secrets must be valid UTF-8 strings".into()))'

Issue 1: the Utf8Error source is dropped via '|_|', so callers get no byte offset and no info about where the input went bad. Diagnostics for binary-input footguns are degraded.

Issue 2: SecretError::DecodeFailed is documented as 'Secret was present but could not be decoded as expected' — i.e. for decoding *retrieved* secrets, not for input validation in put(). Using it for input rejection is a category error.

Fix: include the Utf8Error in the message ('invalid UTF-8 at byte {n}: {e}') and keep DecodeFailed if you accept the slight stretch of meaning, or introduce a more appropriate variant. At minimum, preserve the byte offset.
title: put() UTF-8 validation discards Utf8Error source and uses wrong error variant
